properties.NAME: Grand Valley Shooting Ranges
properties.F_SAFETY_FEATURES: Berms, Buffer Distances
properties.COMMENTS_OR_NOTES: HAZMAT issues, unmanaged; 1978 a 10-year R&PP Lease for a “public shooting range and recreation area” was issued to the Mesa County. A concrete vault toilet was installed at the location but due to vandalism, the structure was removed by BLM. 1988 BLM decision that Mesa County would relinquish the lease.The concrete pads, shade structures and target berms were left.

properties.COMMENTS_OR_NOTES: These are the same range. it appears that when the R&PP lease was first created, they put the actual lease under MTM-07865101 (now serial number MTMT106065423), and the NORA for this R&PP lease (Notice of Realty Action) was filed under MTM-078651.  The only shooting range "site" is the R&PP lease under MTM-7865101 or MTMT106065423.

properties.NAME: Prairie County Shooting Range
properties.F_SAFETY_FEATURES: Other Public Safety Design or Features (only)
properties.COMMENTS_OR_NOTES: Original Special Land Use permit was applied for by Prairie County in 1978 to use land for shooting/rifle range; Outdoor toilets and a wooden storage building were originally installed but have since been removed. Another Land Use Permit was completed in 1989 and 1995. A Right of Way grant was filed in 1999 and then again in 2019. All information can be found in the Realty File MTM #80587. NEPA analysis CX-MT-C020-2019-0057

properties.NAME: Southern Utah Shooting Sports Complex
properties.F_SAFETY_FEATURES: Berms, Buffer Distances, Other Public Safety Design or Features
properties.COMMENTS_OR_NOTES: Managed by Washington County Parks. This is a highly developed shooting park that supports the general public, along with several shooting clubs and groups. The site hosts competetive shooting events. Facilities include several outdoor short and long ranges for pistol and rifle shooting, skeet shooting trails, an archery range, shade-covered bays, electronic target systems, and volunteer staff to provide safety instructions and other assistance. More info: https://shootingsportspark.com/

properties.COMMENTS_OR_NOTES: Range is managed by Uintah County. Shooting oppertunities include Long Distance Range (up to a mile), Buffalo Longdistance (1500 yards), Cowboy Action Range, 200 Yard Bays, 360 Pistol Range Bays, 100 Yard Ranges, and Sporting Clays. (https://www.buckskinhills.com/shootingrange)

properties.NAME: Emery County Shooting Range
properties.F_SAFETY_FEATURES: Buffer Distances, Other Public Safety Design or Features
properties.COMMENTS_OR_NOTES: The range, managed by Emery County, is primarily set up for trap shooting. It features three concrete bunkers for throwers and clay targets, walkways, benches, shade structures, lights, and a converted shed used as a clubhouse. Portable toilets and garbage cans are available year-round. The Sheriff's Office uses a short-range facility on-site for training. While long-range rifle and archery setups have been used occasionally, they are not primary uses.

properties.NAME: Blanding Shooting Range
properties.F_SAFETY_FEATURES: Berms, Buffer Distances, Other Public Safety Design or Features
properties.COMMENTS_OR_NOTES: BLM Monticello is interested in funding for replacing damaged shooting benches. This range consists of one 100+ yard range with designated firing line and two short-range/pistol areas. The short range areas do not have designated firing lines. The BLM Monticello Office is interested in improving the infrastructure at this location. If funding or assistance is available, please contact Jeremy Martin j4martin@blm.gov.
